amenagement.title: Spatial Planning – How Luxembourg Plans the Use of Its Land
amenagement.description: What spatial planning ('aménagement du territoire') is, how the State and communes plan Luxembourg's limited land for the long term — balancing housing, transport, the economy and nature — through the national master programme and sectoral plans, working across regions and borders, and how citizens can follow and take part.

questions.text: What is spatial planning ("aménagement du territoire")?
options: A way to design house interiors
How the State and communes plan the use of the country's land for the long term
questions.explanation: Spatial planning is the set of actions by the State and the communes to develop the national territory wisely — balancing housing, transport, the economy and nature, protecting landscapes and using the soil rationally.

questions.text: Why does spatial planning matter so much in Luxembourg?
options: Because the country has unlimited land
Because land is limited and many needs compete for it
Because it is only about tourism
questions.explanation: Luxembourg is small, so land is precious. Housing, transport, the economy and nature all need space. Planning coordinates these competing needs so the country grows in a balanced, sustainable way.

questions.text: What are the sectoral master plans?
options: Coordinated plans that reserve and protect land for transport, housing, economic zones and landscapes
questions.explanation: The sectoral master plans are regulatory plans that decide, in a coordinated way, what land is for — for example reserving land for housing near transport, or protecting green landscapes from being built over.

questions.text: What is a key principle of good spatial planning?
options: Concentrating homes and activity near transport and services
questions.explanation: Building where it makes sense — concentrating homes and activity near transport and services rather than spreading out everywhere — protects the countryside, reduces traffic and makes daily life easier.

questions.text: How can citizens take part in spatial planning?
options: Through public consultations when plans affect their area
questions.explanation: Big plans often go through public consultation. Citizens can inform themselves and, when a plan affects their commune, give their view. The portal publishes the strategy, plans and news openly.

aaa.title: The Accident Insurance (AAA) – Cover If You Are Hurt at Work or School
aaa.description: What the Association d'assurance accident (AAA) is — Luxembourg's public accident insurance — what it covers (accidents at work, commuting accidents, school accidents and occupational diseases), the care and compensation it provides, how it is funded with a bonus-malus system, its work on prevention, and how accidents are declared.

questions.text: What is the AAA?
options: Luxembourg's public accident insurance, part of social security
questions.explanation: The AAA, the Association d'assurance accident, is Luxembourg's public accident insurance and part of the social security system. It is there for you automatically.

questions.text: Which of these does the AAA cover?
options: Accidents at work, on the way to work, at school, and occupational diseases
questions.explanation: The AAA covers accidents at work, commuting accidents (on your normal way to or from work), accidents at school and around school activities, and occupational diseases caused by your work.

questions.text: What does the AAA do for you after a covered accident?
options: It covers medical care and provides compensation for lasting effects or loss of income
questions.explanation: The AAA takes care of the costs and consequences of the accident — including medical care, compensation if you can't work or there are lasting effects, and support for the family in the most serious cases.

questions.text: How is the accident insurance mainly funded?
options: By employer contributions, with a bonus-malus system
questions.explanation: The insurance is funded mainly by employers through contributions, with a bonus-malus system that rewards workplaces with fewer accidents — encouraging everyone to take safety seriously.

questions.text: What must happen for the AAA to act on an accident?
options: Nothing — it is automatic with no paperwork
The accident must be declared, often by the employer, increasingly via MyGuichet
You must pay a fee first
questions.explanation: The accident must be declared to the AAA. For accidents at work, your employer normally makes the declaration, and much of this can now be done electronically through MyGuichet.

geoportail.title: The Geoportal – Luxembourg's Official Maps, Online and Free
geoportail.description: What geoportail.lu is — Luxembourg's official, free national map platform run by the cadastre and topography administration — the maps and layers it offers (topography, aerial photos, historic maps, the land registry and many themes), its tools to search, measure, view in 3D and print, and its open data for new apps.

questions.text: What is geoportail.lu?
options: Luxembourg's official, free online map platform
questions.explanation: Geoportail.lu is the official national platform for the State's geographic data and maps, run by the cadastre and topography administration. The maps are accurate, trustworthy and free to use.

questions.text: What can you see on the Geoportal?
options: Standard maps, aerial photos, historic maps, the land registry and many thematic layers
questions.explanation: Beyond the standard map, you can view aerial photos, old maps, the land registry (cadastre) showing parcel boundaries, and switchable layers on themes like the environment, mobility and tourism.

questions.text: What does the cadastre (land registry) show?
options: The boundaries of land parcels — who owns what piece of land and where the lines are
questions.explanation: The cadastre shows the boundaries of land parcels. If you are buying a house or curious about a plot, you can look up the parcel and its official boundaries.

questions.text: Which tools does the Geoportal offer?
options: Search an address, measure distances, view in 3D, and print maps
questions.explanation: The Geoportal has handy built-in tools: search for an address, measure a distance or area, explore parts of the country in 3D, and print a clean map for a walk, a project or a meeting.

questions.text: Why is much of the Geoportal's data "open"?
options: So researchers, companies and developers can build new apps and services from it
To sell it at high prices
questions.explanation: A lot of the geographic data is made openly available so that researchers, companies and developers can create new apps and services — public data working for the public good and for innovation.

govcert.title: GOVCERT.LU – Defending Luxembourg Against Cyberattacks
govcert.description: What GOVCERT.LU is — the Government of Luxembourg's cyber incident response team (CSIRT) — how it defends the country's critical systems, responds to and analyses cyberattacks, why cybersecurity is a shared responsibility, how anyone can report an incident, and simple habits to stay safe, especially against phishing.

questions.text: What is GOVCERT.LU?
options: The government's cyber security incident response team (CSIRT)
questions.explanation: GOVCERT.LU is the Computer Security Incident Response Team (CSIRT) of the Government of Luxembourg — like a fire brigade for cyberattacks, responding quickly to serious computer security incidents.

questions.text: Whose systems does GOVCERT.LU mainly protect?
options: The government's systems and other critical public or private infrastructure
questions.explanation: GOVCERT.LU is the central contact point for IT incidents that could compromise the information systems of the government and other critical infrastructure operators — the systems a country cannot function without.

questions.text: According to GOVCERT.LU, cybersecurity is…
options: A collective responsibility we all share
questions.explanation: GOVCERT.LU stresses that cybersecurity is a collective responsibility. That is why they encourage people to report incidents they discover, so they can analyse them and protect others.

questions.text: What is "phishing"?
options: A fake message that tries to trick you into giving passwords, card numbers or money
questions.explanation: Phishing is when a fake message tries to trick you into giving away passwords, card numbers or money. GOVCERT.LU regularly publishes warnings about phishing campaigns circulating in Luxembourg.

questions.text: Which is a good everyday cyber-safety habit?
options: Be suspicious of urgent messages, check links, use strong passwords, and keep devices updated
questions.explanation: Simple habits protect you: be suspicious of urgent messages asking for passwords or payments, check the real address behind a link, use strong and different passwords with two-step verification, and keep devices and apps updated.

culture.title: The Culture Portal – Discovering Cultural Life in Luxembourg
culture.description: What culture.lu is — the cultural portal supported by the Ministry of Culture that gathers Luxembourg's cultural life in one place — its agenda of events across the country, the museums and venues it points to, heritage and special open days, and its support for the cultural sector, making culture accessible to all.

questions.text: What is culture.lu?
options: The cultural portal gathering Luxembourg's cultural life in one place
questions.explanation: Culture.lu is a website, supported by the Ministry of Culture, that gathers the essential information about cultural life in Luxembourg — a single, welcoming doorway to the cultural sector.

questions.text: What is at the heart of the culture portal?
options: An agenda listing cultural events across the country
questions.explanation: At its heart is the agenda — a calendar bringing together concerts, plays, exhibitions, festivals and family activities from across the whole country, so you can easily find something to do.

questions.text: Does the portal cover only the capital city?
options: No — it covers cultural life across the whole country
questions.explanation: The portal is for the whole country. Culture happens in towns and villages everywhere, and it helps you find venues and events near you, not only in the capital.

questions.text: Besides current events, what else does the portal highlight?
options: Heritage and special open days, like museum nights
questions.explanation: The portal also points to heritage — castles, traditions and history — and to special events such as museum nights and heritage days, when doors open to everyone.

questions.text: Who else is the culture portal for, besides audiences?
options: The people who make culture — artists, institutions and organisers
questions.explanation: The portal also supports the cultural sector itself — artists, institutions and organisers — as part of a wider effort to develop culture and make it more accessible, inclusive and present in every region.

demenz.title: Info-Zenter Demenz – Help and Hope Around Dementia
demenz.description: What the Info-Zenter Demenz is — Luxembourg's free national information and advice centre on memory problems and dementia — who it helps, how it explains dementia and diagnosis, the support it offers people affected and their relatives, and how a healthy lifestyle can lower the risk.

questions.text: What is the Info-Zenter Demenz?
options: Luxembourg's national information and advice centre on memory problems and dementia
questions.explanation: The Info-Zenter Demenz is the national centre for information and advice on memory problems and dementia, for those affected, their relatives, health professionals and anyone with questions.

questions.text: How much do the centre's services cost?
options: Only the first visit is free
questions.explanation: All of the centre's advice and materials are free, so cost is never a barrier to getting good information and support.

questions.text: What is an important message about memory and dementia?
options: Dementia is not normal ageing, and ordinary memory lapses are different from dementia
questions.explanation: Dementia is not a normal part of ageing, and not every forgotten name is dementia. The centre helps explain the difference, and how a proper diagnosis works — so the right step is good information, not panic.

questions.text: Who does the centre support?
options: The person affected and their relatives and carers
questions.explanation: The centre supports both the person affected — with guidance on living well, work, driving and planning ahead — and the relatives who care for them, helping carers look after their own health too.

questions.text: Can the risk of dementia be reduced?
options: Yes — a healthy lifestyle can lower the risk
questions.explanation: Living healthily — staying active, eating well, keeping the mind and social life busy — can reduce the risk. It is not a guarantee, but what is good for the heart is also good for the brain.

ess.title: The Social and Solidarity Economy – Business with a Purpose
ess.description: What the social and solidarity economy is — organisations whose first aim is a social or societal benefit rather than only profit, such as cooperatives, associations, foundations and social enterprises — Luxembourg's 'societal impact company' status, and how the portal helps people create a business that does good.

questions.text: What defines the social and solidarity economy?
options: Making as much profit as possible
Organisations whose first purpose is a social or societal benefit, not just profit
questions.explanation: The social and solidarity economy is the world of organisations whose first aim is a social or societal benefit. Profit still matters for survival, but it is a means, not the main goal.

questions.text: Which of these belong to the social and solidarity economy?
options: Cooperatives, associations, mutual societies, foundations and social enterprises
questions.explanation: It includes cooperatives, associations, mutual societies, foundations and social enterprises — organisations you may already know, which create real jobs and services but reinvest in their mission.

questions.text: What is the "societal impact company"?
options: A legal status for a business whose official purpose is social impact
questions.explanation: Luxembourg created a legal status, the societal impact company (société d'impact sociétal), which lets you build a real business whose official purpose is social impact, recognised by law.

questions.text: What does the social and solidarity economy often do for people?
options: Creates jobs and serves needs like inclusion, care and the environment
questions.explanation: The sector creates jobs — including for people who find it hard to enter the normal job market — and serves needs that ordinary business may overlook, such as inclusion, care and the environment.

questions.text: How does the portal help someone with an idea?
options: It explains the social economy and how to create such a company
questions.explanation: The portal explains the social and solidarity economy and how to create this kind of company — so if you dream of starting a business that also makes a difference, it shows you the path.

luxinnovation.title: Luxinnovation – Helping Businesses Innovate and Grow
luxinnovation.description: What Luxinnovation is — Luxembourg's national innovation agency — how it helps companies, startups and research organisations innovate and grow, its programmes like the Fit 4 Start startup accelerator, its guidance on national and European funding, and how it strengthens whole strategic sectors by connecting business and research.

questions.text: What is Luxinnovation?
questions.explanation: Luxinnovation is Luxembourg's national innovation agency. It helps companies, startups and research organisations to grow, innovate and stay competitive.

questions.text: Who does Luxinnovation help?
options: Big and small companies, startups and research organisations
questions.explanation: Luxinnovation helps established companies, small firms and brand-new startups, and connects them with the research world — turning clever research into real products, services and jobs.

questions.text: What is Fit 4 Start?
options: A startup acceleration programme offering coaching and early funding
questions.explanation: Fit 4 Start is a startup acceleration programme that selects promising young startups and gives them coaching, support and early funding to grow — invaluable for a founder with a great idea.

questions.text: How does Luxinnovation help with funding?
options: It lends its own money only
It guides companies through national and European funding and how to apply
questions.explanation: There is national funding and big European programmes for research and innovation. Luxinnovation helps companies find their way through this maze and apply for the right support.

questions.text: Why does innovation matter for ordinary residents?
options: It creates jobs, diversifies the economy and improves daily life
questions.explanation: Innovation creates good, future-proof jobs, diversifies the economy so the country isn't reliant on one sector, and produces things that improve daily life — and a thriving economy funds public services.

logement.title: The Housing Portal – Help to Rent, Buy and Renovate in Luxembourg
logement.description: What the housing portal (logement.public.lu) offers — the Ministry of Housing's one-stop site for tenants, owners and communes — tenants' rights and rental help like the rent subsidy, support for buying, building and renovating a home, the single counter for housing aids, and the push for affordable housing.

questions.text: What is logement.public.lu?
options: The Ministry of Housing's one-stop housing portal
questions.explanation: Logement.public.lu is the Ministry of Housing's portal, organised for tenants, owners and communes. It gathers in one place everything about housing — renting, buying, renovating and the aids the State offers.

questions.text: What financial help for renters does the portal explain?
options: A rent subsidy for households on lower incomes
questions.explanation: The portal explains help for renters, including a rent subsidy for households on lower incomes — support many people who would qualify don't even know exists.

questions.text: What support does the portal cover for owners and buyers?
options: Help with buying or building a home, and reduced registration costs for a main residence
questions.explanation: For owners and buyers, the portal sets out support linked to buying or building a home, including a well-known measure that reduces the registration costs when you buy a home to live in.

questions.text: What does the single counter for housing aids do?
options: Lets you ask about and apply for the different housing supports in one place
questions.explanation: There is a single counter for housing aids — one office where you can ask about, and apply for, the different housing supports, so you don't have to run between many offices.

questions.text: What national goal does the housing policy pursue?
options: Building more affordable housing, in partnership with the communes
questions.explanation: The portal explains the policy on affordable housing — building more homes that ordinary people can actually afford — and the partnership with communes to create affordable homes locally.

space.title: The Luxembourg Space Agency – A Small Country Reaching for the Stars
space.description: What the Luxembourg Space Agency is and how Luxembourg became a space hub since 1985, the government support and pioneering space laws behind its growth, how space technology powers everyday life (navigation, communications, climate and disaster monitoring), the focus on doing space sustainably, and the jobs and inspiration it brings.

questions.text: What does the Luxembourg Space Agency do?
options: Develops and supports Luxembourg's space sector
questions.explanation: The Luxembourg Space Agency develops and supports the country's space sector — helping companies, funding and talent, attracting space businesses, and connecting Luxembourg to partners like the European Space Agency.

questions.text: When did Luxembourg first enter the space sector?
options: In 1985, with the creation of the satellite company SES
questions.explanation: Luxembourg's space story began in 1985, when it helped create the satellite company SES, which became a world leader in communications from space. A whole space sector has grown from that seed.

questions.text: How has Luxembourg's space sector changed in recent years?
options: It has grown a lot — to dozens of companies and over a thousand jobs
questions.explanation: In just over a decade, the sector grew from a handful of companies and a few hundred people to dozens of companies and well over a thousand jobs, thanks to government support and pioneering space laws.

questions.text: How does space technology touch everyday life?
options: Through navigation, communications, weather, and climate and disaster monitoring
questions.explanation: Space quietly powers daily life: satellite navigation, communications, weather forecasts, and Earth observation that tracks the climate, pollution and floods, and helps respond to disasters and support farming.

questions.text: What does Luxembourg's space strategy emphasise?
questions.explanation: Luxembourg's strategy stresses sustainability — keeping space usable for future generations and using space to help solve challenges on Earth — a mature way to approach a powerful new frontier.
